gap_summaries.description: Task 10: Doctor test uses nonexistent methods (self._run_doctor, self.project_root). test_doctor.py uses module-level _run(args) and has no temp-dir setup. Entire test code block was incompatible with the existing harness.
gap_summaries.fix: Rewrote Task 10 tests to use existing _run([]) pattern, _EXPECTED_CHECKS set, and DoctorCommandTests class methods.

gap_summaries.description: Task 10: Doctor output structure mismatch. Plan expected result["profile"]["profile_id"] but actual output is {"ok": bool, "checks": [...], "summary": {...}} using _check() dicts. No "profile" top-level key exists.
gap_summaries.fix: Replaced _profile_preflight with _check_profile() following the _check(name, status, detail) pattern. Profile data appears as a check entry in the checks list.

gap_summaries.description: Task 8: Tests didn't specify which class to add methods to. test_runtime_policy.py has RuntimePolicyTests with _write_override and load_effective_policy in scope; plan said 'append' without naming the target class.
gap_summaries.fix: Added explicit instruction: 'Append the following 4 methods to the existing RuntimePolicyTests class'.

gap_summaries.description: Task 8: _validate_policy_shape insertion point 'after _validate_security_shape' is at line 316, but lines 317-356 contain snapshot/runtime/workflow/steps validation. Adding profile/gate checks mid-function is misleading.
gap_summaries.fix: Changed to 'at the end of _validate_policy_shape (after the for-loop over steps, around line 356)'.

gap_summaries.description: Task 10: _profile_preflight(project_root) takes project_root parameter but cmd_doctor(args) doesn't have one. Doctor uses its own _project_root() helper at line 31.
gap_summaries.fix: _check_profile() now calls load_effective_profile(_project_root()) using doctor's own helper.

gap_summaries.description: Task 10: Plan imported command_exists from utils.py but doctor.py uses shutil.which directly for all binary checks. Inconsistent with established pattern.
Task 10: _EXPECTED_CHECKS in test_doctor.py didn't include 'profile'. Adding _check_profile() to doctor without updating _EXPECTED_CHECKS would fail test_emits_valid_json_with_all_checks.
Task 9: MSME-ERP profile uses 'typescript' as toolchain key but spec §5 shows 'ts'. Toolchain keys are opaque identifiers so both work, but downstream consumers must match the chosen convention.
gap_summaries.note: Keeping 'typescript' as it's more explicit. The spec YAML is shorthand. Profile conventions are being established by this milestone.

gap_summaries.description: Task 4 Step 1: 'Add import os' instruction is prose not a code block. An implementer could miss it among the test code.
gap_summaries.note: Low risk since the test will fail with NameError: name 'os' is not defined, making the fix obvious.

gap_summaries.description: Task 10 test_profile_check_present is a minimal smoke test. No test for the missing-tool warn path because it requires a temp project root with override file + PROJECT_ROOT env var mock, which is more complex than the existing doctor test harness supports.
gap_summaries.note: Deferred to implementation — implementer can add a more thorough test with PROJECT_ROOT mocking if needed. The missing-tool path is exercised by the MSME-ERP profile in CI where tools are absent.
